2606.30556.title: Poller: Are LLMs Suitable for Evaluating the Poetry Understanding Task?
2606.30556.paper_summary: Traditional automatic evaluation methods have been shown to be unsuitable for modern Chinese poetry because of the distinct nature of this literary genre. Human evaluation remains reliable, but is expensive and not applicable to large-scale data. In this paper, we propose Poller (Poetry LLM Evaluator), a novel method leveraging large language models (LLMs) to evaluate the poetry understanding task. Specifically, our method requires LLMs to play the role of a poem's author with detailed information, thereby emulating human evaluation and judgment by adopting the poet's perspective. We conducted comprehensive experiments on multiple LLMs, evaluating the interpretations of poems across eight specialized dimensions. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively reduces the evaluation error between LLMs and humans. Especially for specific dimension evaluation, Poller-based LLMs achieve a 94.55% and 89.53% error reduction for rhetorical techniques and defamiliarization, respectively, compared to baseline methods. These performances are unattainable by conventional LLM evaluation methods. Experimental results from multiple LLMs across various dimensions validate the efficacy of our method. This work bridges the gap between automated efficiency and human expertise, establishing a foundation for automated evaluation in poetry-related tasks.
2606.30556.paper_summary_zh: 傳統的自動評估方法已被證明不適用於現代中文詩歌，因為這一文學類型具有獨特的特性。人類評估仍然可靠，但成本高昂且不適用於大規模數據。在本文中，我們提出了Poller（詩歌LLM評估器），這是一種利用大型語言模型（LLMs）來評估詩歌理解任務的新方法。具體而言，我們的方法要求LLMs扮演詩人的角色，並提供詳細信息，從而通過採用詩人的視角來模擬人類的評估和判斷。我們在多個LLMs上進行了全面的實驗，評估詩歌在八個專業維度上的詮釋。實驗結果表明，我們的方法有效減少了LLMs與人類之間的評估誤差。特別是在特定維度評估方面，基於Poller的LLMs在修辭技術和陌生化方面分別實現了94.55%和89.53%的誤差減少，相較於基準方法。這些表現是傳統LLM評估方法無法達到的。來自多個LLMs在各個維度上的實驗結果驗證了我們方法的有效性。本研究彌合了自動化效率與人類專業知識之間的差距，為詩歌相關任務的自動評估建立了基礎。
2606.30556.author: Shanshan Wang et.al.
2606.30556.authors: Shanshan Wang, Derek F. Wong, Jingming Yao, Lidia S. Chao
